paper_title: Tracing the Spatial Distribution of Whole-Lake Exchange of Groundwater and Lake Water in Low-Hydraulic Gradient Systems Using δ18O and Electrical Conductivity and Uncertain End-Member Mixing Analysis

methods.method_name: δ 18 O and Electrical Conductivity (EC) Sampling and Analysis
methods.description: This method describes the process for collecting shallow groundwater and surface water samples from various locations around the lake, measuring Electrical Conductivity (EC) on-site, and analyzing δ 18 O in the laboratory using Cavity Ring-Down Spectroscopy (CRDS). It also includes steps for sample preparation and obtaining necessary off-site precipitation data.

steps.description: Sample δ 18 O and EC at 30 locations (February 2016) in shallow groundwater in the immediate vicinity of the lake.
steps.searchable_summary: collect shallow groundwater samples for isotope and conductivity analysis

steps.description: Sample Wells TA12 and TA13 on 22-23 June 2015 for assessing the groundwater end-member compositions.
steps.searchable_summary: collect groundwater samples from specific wells for end-member assessment

steps.description: Sample TA18 and surface water in Bøgsted Ditch (BC1-10, Figure 2) on 22 August 2017 to confirm lake recharge to groundwater.
steps.searchable_summary: collect groundwater and surface water samples from specific locations for recharge confirmation

steps.description: Sample surface water in the lake (two samples).
steps.searchable_summary: collect surface water samples from the lake

steps.description: Drive mini-piezometers (diameter of 0.5 centimeter and a 5-centimeter screen) into the subsurface at locations 1-30 by use of a hammer or a small drill to a depth of 1.25 m below the surface.
steps.searchable_summary: install mini-piezometers for shallow groundwater sampling

steps.description: Filter samples (0.7 μm nominal pore size Whatman GF/F filters) the same day.
steps.searchable_summary: filter water samples using GF/F filters

steps.description: Store filtered samples in a cooler.
steps.searchable_summary: store filtered water samples in a cooler

steps.description: Analyze δ 18 O in the samples at the Geological Survey of Denmark and Greenland (GEUS) with a PICARRO L2021 using cavity ring-down spectroscopy (CRDS).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze δ18O isotope ratios using CRDS

steps.description: Express all values for isotopes in per-mill (‰) with the δ-notation indicating the deviation from the VSMOW (Vienna Standard Mean Ocean Water).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate and express isotope values in per-mill δ-notation

steps.description: Measure EC on-site using a YSI Model 85 conductivity meter (YSI Incorporated, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: measure electrical conductivity (EC) on-site

steps.description: Obtain local data on δ 18 O in precipitation (2012-2016) from an off-site coastal station (Skjern Enge) [23].
steps.searchable_summary: acquire off-site precipitation δ18O data
outputs: precipitation δ18O data (mean, max, min, seasonality)

steps.description: Obtain EC in precipitation (2011-2012) from an inland field station (Voulund) [24], excluding three very high concentrations (108-211 µS/cm).
steps.searchable_summary: acquire off-site precipitation EC data and filter outliers

methods.method_name: End-Member Mixing Analysis using MIX Code
methods.description: This method utilizes the MIX code [20] to compute mixing fractions and estimate end-member concentrations (precipitation, groundwater, lake water) based on δ 18 O and EC data. The method accounts for uncertainty in both end-member and sample concentrations using a maximum likelihood approach.

steps.description: Choose three end-members: precipitation, groundwater, and lake water.
steps.searchable_summary: define end-members for mixing model

steps.description: Assess initial end-member concentrations for δ 18 O and EC and associated uncertainty using data presented in Sections 3.1 and 3.2 (Table 1) and off-site data.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate initial end-member concentrations and uncertainties from collected and external data
inputs: collected δ18O and EC data (Sections 3.1, 3.2, Table 1)

steps.description: Evaluate both EC and δ 18 O concentrations for the groundwater end-member based on concentrations from a depth greater than 6 mbs (Figure 5) in wells TA12 and TA13.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate groundwater end-member concentrations using data from specific depths in wells
inputs: groundwater data from wells TA12 and TA13
outputs: refined initial groundwater end-member concentration estimate

steps.description: Assign uncertainty (σ) to the initially assumed end-member concentrations (e.g., σ = 1.57‰ for precipitation δ 18 O).
steps.searchable_summary: assign uncertainty values to initial end-member concentrations
inputs: calculated standard deviations (e.g., 1.57‰ for precipitation δ18O)
outputs: assigned uncertainty values (variance/std dev) for end-members

steps.description: Assign an uncertainty to each sample from the 1-30 sampling locations related to the accuracy by which the samples can be analyzed in the field (EC) or the laboratory (δ 18 O), e.g., 0.08‰ for δ 18 O and 10% uncertainty on the maximum mean EC (20 µS/cm).
steps.searchable_summary: assign uncertainty values to individual sample concentrations based on analytical accuracy
outputs: assigned uncertainty values (std dev) for sample concentrations

steps.description: Use the MIX code by [20] to compute mixing fractions and improved estimates of end-member concentrations.
steps.searchable_summary: run end-member mixing model (MIX code) with uncertain end-members and samples
inputs: initial guesses for mixing fractions and end-member concentrations

steps.description: Run the analysis using single (either δ 18 O or EC) or dual (both) tracers.
steps.searchable_summary: configure mixing model to use single or dual tracers
outputs: model results for specified tracer combinations

steps.description: Obtain final estimates of the end-member concentrations from the MIX code output (Table 2 and Figure 8).
steps.searchable_summary: extract final end-member concentration estimates from model output
